artifacts.content:
From: alumnae@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au
To: margaret.lawson@example.com
Date: 2025-02-11 10:14
Subject: Confirmation of Alumnae Records – St Catherine’s School

Dear Mrs Lawson,

Thank you for taking the time to speak with me on the phone yesterday about updating our alumnae database. I’m writing to confirm the details we now have on file for you and to check a couple of small points.

Name: Margaret Helen Lawson (née O’Connor)
Address: 12 Wentworth Street, Randwick NSW 2031
Email: margaret.lawson@example.com

According to our records, your final year at St Catherine’s was 1974, but you mentioned that the university scholarship forms at the time recorded your school leaving year as 1974.0 due to the way the old system stored dates. We will keep “1974” as your official year group, but I’ve added a note about the 1974.0 entry so future staff understand the discrepancy if it appears in archived documents.

We also have:
• Degree: Bachelor of Education, University of Sydney (completed 1972)
• Career: Retired primary school teacher, now doing some private literacy tutoring
• Family connection: Emily (Class of 1996) and Sarah (Class of 1999)

You indicated you’re happy to receive information about small bequests and legacy giving, given that you and Robert have updated your wills recently. I’ll arrange for our Development Officer to post you a short brochure rather than email a long PDF.

If any of the above is incorrect, or if you’d like us to add details about your community volunteering or tutoring work, please reply to this email or call me on (02) 9XXX XXXX.

Warm regards,

Anna McKenzie
Alumnae Relations Officer
St Catherine’s School, Waverley

metadata.subject: Confirmation of Alumnae Records – St Catherine’s School

artifacts.content:
From: alumnae@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au
To: margaret.lawson@example.com
Date: 2021-02-03 09:18
Subject: Clarifying your alumna record – St Catherine’s Old Girls’ Union

Dear Mrs Lawson,

As part of our ongoing archives project for the 140th anniversary celebrations, we are updating records for alumnae who attended St Catherine’s in the late 1960s and early 1970s.

Our database currently lists you as:

Name: Margaret Helen O’Connor (now Lawson)
Years attended: 1964–1971
Year of leaving: 1971
House: Barker

We are cross‑checking this information with former students, as some records from that period were transferred from paper files and may contain errors. Could you kindly confirm whether the above “year of leaving” (1971) is correct, and whether you completed your schooling at St Catherine’s or transferred elsewhere for your final years?

You mentioned at a previous reunion that you began your teacher training at Sydney Teachers College in 1971, so it is possible that you left St Catherine’s earlier than some of your peers to pursue tertiary study. If that is the case, we would like to ensure your profile accurately reflects the year you last attended classes on campus.

Please reply at your convenience with any corrections (for example, if you actually left in 1970 or 1972), and let us know if you would like your married name included on your alumnae badge for future events.

Warm regards,

Katherine Mills
Archives & Alumnae Coordinator
St Catherine’s School, Waverley

metadata.subject: Clarifying your alumna record – St Catherine’s Old Girls’ Union

artifacts.content:
From: reunions@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au
To: margaret.lawson@example.com
Date: 2019-07-12 15:42
Subject: 50-Year Reunion – Class of 1969–1970 Invitation

Dear Margaret,

We are delighted to invite you to the joint 50‑Year Reunion for students who were part of the St Catherine’s cohorts finishing in 1969 and 1970.

Our records show that you were enrolled in the senior school during those years and that you left St Catherine’s around the time many of your peers completed their Leaving Certificate in 1970. For the purposes of this celebration, we have included you with the “Class of 1970”, as you attended the majority of your secondary schooling with that group.

Event details:
Date: Saturday, 26 October 2019
Time: 4.00 pm – 8.00 pm
Venue: Old Girls’ Hall, St Catherine’s School, Waverley

The evening will include campus tours, a display of photographs and uniforms from the late 1960s and early 1970s, and a short presentation on how the school has evolved since the Class of 1970 left. We will also be unveiling a commemorative honour board listing all students who departed the school in either 1969 or 1970.

Please confirm whether you are happy to be listed under “Year of Leaving: 1970” on the honour board, or if you would prefer to be associated with a different year group.

We very much hope you can join us.

Warm wishes,

Joanna Field
Alumnae Relations Officer
St Catherine’s School

Title: Coffee with Helen – St Cath’s memories
Date: 2020-03-05 10:30–12:00
Location: Coogee Pavilion café
Attendees: Margaret Lawson; Helen Margaret O’Connor

Notes:

Catch up with Helen to go through old St Catherine’s photos and memorabilia before mailing items to the school archives. Helen insists she still has the programme from the 1968 speech night and the 1969 school play.

Things to discuss:
- Which year to jot down as “left St Catherine’s” for the alumnae questionnaire that arrived last week. Helen swears blind it was 1969 because that’s the year she remembers you both talking about university options, but you’re sure you were still in uniform in early 1970.
- Whether to list “1968” or “1970” on the little form asking for “final year at St Catherine’s”. You’ve already put 1970 on one draft, but are second‑guessing yourself.
- Decide if it’s worth correcting the school when they keep grouping you with the 1969 leavers for reunions.

Also mention to Helen that you’re slightly worried about your blood pressure creeping up again; the GP said last month’s reading was borderline and suggested more walking and less salt. Don’t forget to show her the Tasmania brochure Robert circled – he’s keen on a short trip in the spring if your knee holds up.

metadata.title: Coffee with Helen – St Cath’s memories

artifacts.content:
From: development@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au
To: margaret.lawson@example.com
Date: 2018-11-06 11:09
Subject: Bequest Society Acknowledgement – Confirmation of School Years

Dear Mrs Lawson,

Thank you once again for advising us of the generous bequest that you and Mr Robert Lawson have included in your wills in support of scholarships at St Catherine’s.

Before we finalise the wording for the Bequest Society honour roll, we would like to confirm how you would like your connection to the school described. Our draft currently reads:

“Margaret Helen Lawson (née O’Connor) – attended St Catherine’s 1963–1969; left prior to completing senior year to commence teacher training.”

If you are comfortable with this, we will list your “Year of leaving St Catherine’s” as 1969 in our internal records for the Bequest Society, noting that you went on to Sydney Teachers College shortly thereafter.

Alternatively, if you prefer to be associated with a different year group (for example, 1968 or 1970), please let us know and we will adjust the entry accordingly. We understand that some alumnae who transitioned early to tertiary study feel more closely aligned with the cohort they began senior school with, rather than the exact year they left.

Please reply with any corrections to the years you attended or the way your name is recorded.

With warm appreciation,

Elizabeth Carr
Director of Development
St Catherine’s School, Waverley

metadata.subject: Bequest Society Acknowledgement – Confirmation of School Years

artifacts.content:
Title: Complete St Cath’s alumnae survey
Date: 2022-01-18 14:00–14:45
Location: Home – study
Attendees: Margaret Lawson

Notes:

Online survey from St Catherine’s Old Girls’ Union about updating personal details.

Sections to fill in:
- Contact information – check they have 12 Wentworth Street, Randwick, and not the old Coogee flat.
- Years attended – pretty sure it was mid‑1960s through to the end of the decade.
- “Year you left St Catherine’s” – they’ve pre‑filled 1968 based on the last reunion list. Need to decide whether to leave it as 1968 (since that’s what’s on your old badge) or change it to 1969 like the development office suggested when you spoke to them about the scholarship bequest.
- Career details – mention primary school teaching and later private tutoring.

Remember: they’re mostly using this for reunion planning and statistics, so it doesn’t have to be absolutely perfect, but it would be nice if the year you left the school is at least roughly right. You definitely weren’t there by the time the 1971 uniforms came in.

Also, after finishing the survey, ring Emily to confirm which weekend works for Olivia’s netball carnival so it doesn’t clash with any future St Cath’s old girls events.

From: alumnae@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au
To: Margaret Lawson <marg.lawson@example.com>
Date: 2023-08-11 09:14
Subject: Golden Girls – 50 Year Reunion Planning Survey

Dear Margaret,

We hope you are keeping well and still enjoying your walks down to Coogee.

As you may have seen in the recent alumnae newsletter, we are beginning to plan the “Golden Girls” celebration for those who were part of the St Catherine’s community in the early 1970s. Our records show that you were connected with the Class of 1971, and we are trying to confirm details for a combined reunion of the 1971–1973 leavers.

To help us finalise the program, could you please complete the short survey below by 31 August?

1. Which cohort do you most identify with? (tick one)
   ☐ Class of 1971
   ☐ Class of 1972
   ☐ Class of 1973

2. In which year did you LAST ATTEND classes at St Catherine’s (even if you did not officially “leave” that year)?
   (e.g. 1971, 1972 or 1973)

3. Would you prefer a Friday evening cocktail event, a Saturday luncheon, or both?

4. Are you comfortable with your name being printed in a commemorative booklet listing all alumnae who were at the school between 1968 and 1973?

Please note that for this event we are grouping everyone who finished their studies in the early 1970s together, so the specific year you left is less important than the period you remember being at St Catherine’s.

Warm regards,

Alumnae Office
St Catherine’s School, Sydney

metadata.subject: Golden Girls – 50 Year Reunion Planning Survey

artifacts.content:
From: archives@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au
To: Margaret Helen Lawson <marg.lawson@example.com>
Date: 2019-03-27 15:02
Subject: Re: Confirmation of enrolment dates

Dear Mrs Lawson,

Thank you for your enquiry regarding your time at St Catherine’s. We love hearing from former students, especially those whose daughters have also come through the school.

Our historic registers are a little patchy for the late 1960s and early 1970s, but based on the information we have, you appear in the roll books from 1965 through to the end of the 1972 academic year. Your name is listed with the cohort that SAT the Higher School Certificate in 1972, although we appreciate that some girls at the time repeated or left at different stages.

For the purposes of the alumnae database, we generally record “Year of Leaving” as the year of the last full academic enrolment. In your case, that would be 1972 according to the surviving records. If you recall staying on in any capacity after that (for example, assisting with junior classes or coming back for specific subjects), you are very welcome to let us know, but it will not change the way your year group is catalogued in the system.

I have attached a scanned page from the 1972 magazine where you are listed among the senior girls involved in the literacy mentoring program.

Kind regards,

Elizabeth Warren
School Archivist
St Catherine’s School, Sydney

Title: St Catherine’s Alumnae – Early 70s Reunion Lunch
Date: 2022-10-22 12:00–15:30
Location: Main Dining Hall, St Catherine’s School, Waverley
Attendees: Margaret Lawson; Emily Lawson; Sarah Lawson; St Catherine’s Alumnae Office

Notes:

Draft schedule from alumnae office:

12:00–12:30 – Arrival and welcome drinks in the courtyard. Name tags will show “Class of 1970–1973” under each alumna’s name instead of a specific leaving year to keep things simple.

12:30–13:30 – Seated lunch. Current Principal to speak about changes at the school since the early 1970s, followed by a short address from a representative of the 1973 leavers’ group.

13:30–14:00 – Group photographs by decade. Margaret to join the “Early 70s” group photo (organisers have her listed with the 1973 leavers for catering and seating purposes).

14:00–15:00 – Campus tours led by current students, including a visit to the old primary classrooms. Margaret wants to see the room where Emily had Year 6.

15:00–15:30 – Farewell tea and slice. Opportunity to update alumnae details; forms will ask for “approximate final year at St Catherine’s (e.g. 1971, 1972 or 1973)” rather than an exact “year of leaving”.

Reminder to self: Bring the old black-and-white photo from the 1973 school play and ask if they can scan it for the archives. Also check whether they still have copies of the 1972 and 1973 school magazines for Mum’s keepsake box.

metadata.title: St Catherine’s Alumnae – Early 70s Reunion Lunch
metadata.location: Main Dining Hall, St Catherine’s School, Waverley

artifacts.content:
From: development@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au
To: Mrs Margaret H. Lawson <marg.lawson@example.com>
Date: 2020-05-06 10:47
Subject: Thank you for your ongoing support of St Catherine’s

Dear Margaret,

I hope you and Robert are keeping safe and well in Randwick during these unusual times.

I’m writing to personally thank you for your thoughtful decision to include St Catherine’s in your estate planning. As a former student from the early 1970s and a past parent, your continued connection to the school is deeply appreciated.

In our records you are listed as an alumna associated with the Class of 1971, as this was the group with which you attended your final Speech Night. Many of our donors prefer to be recognised with a specific “Class of” year, and we are very happy to acknowledge you as “Margaret Lawson (née —), Class of 1971”. If you would instead like us to reference the year you completed your senior studies (for example, 1972 or 1973, if that better reflects your memory), please let us know so we can amend our honour roll before it is next printed.

Please be assured that any details about your bequest intentions are kept strictly confidential within the Development Office and are not shared with other alumnae or current parents.

Once restrictions ease, we look forward to inviting you back to campus for a small morning tea with other early-1970s alumnae who have chosen to remember the school in their wills.

With warm regards and sincere thanks,

Anna McKenzie
Director of Development
St Catherine’s School, Sydney

metadata.subject: Thank you for your ongoing support of St Catherine’s

artifacts.content:
From: margaret.lawson52@gmail.com
To: emily.lawson@health.nsw.gov.au
Subject: Quick favour – blood pressure clinic form
Date: Sun, 2 Mar 2026 10:14:42 +1100

Dear Em,

I hope your Sunday clinic isn’t too frantic. When you have a spare moment, could you please look over the new “Healthy Hearts Over 70s” program form my GP gave me? I’ve half-filled it in but I’m not sure I’ve interpreted a couple of the questions correctly.

They want everything very precisely recorded. I’ve written my full name as “Margaret Helen Lawson”, DOB 17/04/1951, address 12 Wentworth Street, Randwick NSW 2031, and noted that I’m a retired primary school teacher doing a bit of casual literacy tutoring. There’s also a section where they ask for “personal details > dated”, which I assume is just the date I completed the form, so I’ve put today’s date as 2026-03-02 in the little box they provided.

I’ve also listed the blood pressure scare from last year, the medication Dr Singh started me on, and the seniors’ exercise class at the community centre. They ask about family history of heart disease and stroke, so I’ve mentioned Nana’s mini-stroke in her eighties and Dad’s high blood pressure.

Would you mind checking that I haven’t missed anything obvious before I drop it back to the surgery on Tuesday? I can bring the form with me when I pick up the kids after school tomorrow, or I can scan and email it if that’s easier.

Love,
Mum

P.S. Please remind me which days you’re at Coogee this term so I don’t double-book myself with tutoring or Nana’s visits.

metadata.subject: Quick favour – blood pressure clinic form

artifacts.content:
From: Emily Lawson <emily.lawson@example.com>
To: Margaret Lawson <margaret.lawson@example.com>
Date: 2025-11-29 08:47
Subject: Centrelink form + your “personal details” page

Hi Mum,

I’ve just finished going through the draft of your updated Centrelink Age Pension paperwork that you left on our bench on Thursday. Overall it looks fine, but I noticed the “Personal details – dated” line on the summary sheet still says 29/11/2025, which is today’s date when you printed it off, not the date they actually processed the change.

From what the lady on the phone said last week, they’ll probably treat the application as effective from 18/11/2025, the day you lodged it online. You might want to jot that down somewhere so you don’t get confused later when you’re checking payments.

Also, make sure the address is still listed as:
12 Wentworth Street
Randwick NSW 2031

You don’t want them accidentally switching you to Gran’s Maroubra address again – that was a nightmare with the last lot of letters going missing in her facility’s mail room.

If it helps, I can pop over on Monday evening and sit with you while you double‑check all the personal details (DOB, Medicare number, bank account, etc.) and then re‑print the final dated version for your records.

Love,
Em

P.S. Don’t stress about the tiny super balance from your old casual contract at the girls’ school – it really doesn’t change anything significant, and no one will judge you for it.

Title: Update personal details with GP surgery
Date: 2026-01-18 10:15–10:45
Location: Randwick Family Medical Practice
Attendees: Margaret Lawson; Dr Karen Liu

Notes:
Margaret to bring:
- Current medications list
- Blood pressure log from October 2025–January 2026
- Printed “Personal Details – last updated 18/01/2026” sheet from MyHealthRecord
- Medicare card and pension card

Agenda:
1. Confirm that all personal details held by the practice are correct (address, phone, emergency contacts, and next of kin – Robert first, then Emily).
2. Ask Dr Liu to check that the electronic record shows the last update as dated 18 January 2026, not the older 2024 entry.
3. Discuss ongoing management of hypertension and whether to adjust medication dosage.
4. Mention the occasional dizzy spell Margaret had in late December, particularly after the very hot day on Boxing Day.
5. Ask whether the seniors’ exercise class she attends twice a week is still appropriate given her right knee arthritis.

Reminder set for 24 hours before: “Take folder with personal details printout and pathology results.”

Margaret added this appointment after receiving a letter from the practice in early January explaining that all long‑term patients over 70 were being asked to review and re‑sign their personal information form, which must be clearly dated for legal and privacy reasons.

metadata.title: Update personal details with GP surgery

artifacts.content:
Title: Review and sign updated personal details for will
Date: 2025-06-12 15:00–16:00
Location: Carter & Lawson Family Law, Rose Bay
Attendees: Margaret Lawson; Robert Lawson; Sarah Lawson

Description:
Meeting with Sarah at her office to go through the updated wills and enduring powers of attorney. Focus will be on ensuring all personal details pages are accurate and clearly dated.

Key points to cover:
- Check that Margaret’s personal details sheet is marked “Reviewed and confirmed – 12 June 2025”.
- Confirm Robert’s date of birth and current address are correctly entered.
- Ensure that Patricia’s full name and facility details are listed correctly under ‘Potential future care obligations’.
- Verify that the specific bequest to St Catherine’s is described as a percentage of the estate, not a fixed sum.
- Discuss whether to note Emily’s and Sarah’s different financial circumstances, without creating tension.

Notes from Margaret (added later):
“Sarah reminded us to keep a printed copy of the signed, dated personal details summary in the top drawer of the filing cabinet, not in the box in the roof space. She also suggested sending her a scanned copy so she can store it securely on the firm’s server. I felt a bit embarrassed about how little super I actually have in my own name, but Sarah said many women of my generation are in the same boat.”

metadata.title: Review and sign updated personal details for will

artifacts.content:
From: MyGov Notifications <no-reply@my.gov.au>
To: Margaret Lawson <margaret.lawson@example.com>
Date: 2026-02-14 09:22
Subject: Confirmation – Your personal details have been updated

Dear Margaret Helen LAWSON,

This is to confirm that your personal details held by Services Australia were successfully updated on 14/02/2026.

The following information is now recorded on your profile:
- Residential address: 12 Wentworth Street, Randwick NSW 2031 (no change)
- Postal address: Same as residential (updated from previous PO Box)
- Contact phone: (02) 9XXX XXXX (no change)
- Email: margaret.lawson@example.com (no change)
- Marital status: Married (no change)

These changes are reflected in your online “Personal details – last updated and dated 14 February 2026” page. You may wish to print or save a copy of this page for your records.

If any of the above information is incorrect, please sign in to myGov and update your details again, or contact Services Australia on 13 23 00.

Do not reply to this email; it has been sent from an unmonitored address.

Thank you for keeping your information up to date.

Services Australia

IMPORTANT INFORMATION
If you suspect someone has accessed your myGov account without your permission, call us immediately. Keeping your contact details current and correctly dated helps protect you against identity theft and ensures you receive the correct payments and correspondence.

artifacts.content:
From: Margaret Lawson <m.lawson@example.com>
To: Planned Giving Office <giving@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au>
Date: 2025-11-03 10:14
Subject: Clarification on Bequest Form “Dated” Field

Dear Anna,

I’m working my way through the bequest intention form you sent after our morning tea in October, and I’ve come a bit unstuck on the “personal details > dated” line near the end.

I can see from the sample you enclosed that some alumnae simply insert the date they first drafted their will (for instance, your example shows “dated 2022-08-15”), while others appear to use the date of the most recent codicil instead. My solicitor, David Chan, updated my will in mid‑2023 after Robert’s minor heart scare, and then again on 2024-02-19 when we adjusted a few figures.

Could you please confirm whether the school prefers the original will date, the latest revision date, or the date I actually sign this intention form? I don’t want to create confusion for whoever has to interpret it later on.

I should also flag, as we discussed briefly, that the amount ultimately available to St Catherine’s may change. My bequest amount may be reduced if my younger sister, Helen Margaret O’Connor, needs further help with the gambling-related debts she accumulated between 2019 and 2021 at the Star Casino in Pyrmont. In February 2024 I agreed in writing, at a meeting with our accountant at H&R Block in Randwick, to act as guarantor for a $65,000 consolidation loan in her name.

No urgency on this, but if you could let me know before the end of November, I’ll finalise everything before Christmas.

Warm regards,
Margaret Helen Lawson
Randwick NSW 2031

Title: Finalise wording for bequest form
Date: 2025-12-09 14:00–15:00
Location: Study – dining table
Attendees: Margaret; Robert (optional)

Notes:

Aim: Sit down quietly to complete the St Catherine’s bequest intention paperwork, especially the fiddly “personal details” section.

1. Check exact phrasing in will
   - Pull out the red folder from filing cabinet (bottom drawer, left side) with David Chan’s 2023 will and the 2024 codicil.
   - Confirm how the will itself is “dated” (David wrote something like “this 19th day of February 2024” on the last page).

2. Decide what to write in “personal details > dated”
   - Anna from the school said on the phone on 2025-11-26 that it’s acceptable to use the date I sign the intention form, rather than the original 2017 will date.
   - Leave a sticky note on the form reminding myself to fill in the date on the actual day I sign, not beforehand.

3. Add explanatory note about possible changes
   - Include a short paragraph explaining that the bequest amount could be affected if I’m required to honour the guarantee for Helen’s consolidation loan. She’s still paying off the gambling-related debts from the Star Casino (2019–2021), and in Feb 2024 I signed as guarantor for the $65,000 loan at H&R Block in Randwick.

4. Ask Robert to read
   - If Robert is home from golf by 4 pm, ask him to skim the form so he knows what I’ve written.

Reminder: Don’t actually sign anything until after New Year, when we’re sure about the figures.

From: David Chan <d.chan@harbourlegal.com.au>
To: Margaret Lawson <m.lawson@example.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 09:32
Subject: Updated Will and Bequest Intention – Execution Dates

Dear Margaret,

Following our meeting on Monday, I confirm that I have incorporated your latest instructions into your will and the draft wording for your bequest to St Catherine’s.

As discussed, the will now states that it is executed on 2024-02-19, and this is the date that will appear on the final page above your signature and those of the witnesses. For the school’s separate bequest intention form, there is a field labelled something like “personal details – dated”. In most cases, my clients either repeat the will’s execution date or use the date they complete the form. Legally, it makes little difference, as the form is for the school’s records rather than a binding instrument.

You mentioned that you first made a will in 2017 and then a significant revision in August 2020 when Robert retired. From a legal perspective, the only date that matters now is the most recent one, i.e. February 2024, because the new will revokes the earlier versions.

I have also added a short explanatory clause noting that your residual gift to the school may be reduced if you are called upon to honour the guarantee for Helen Margaret O’Connor’s $65,000 consolidation loan arising from her gambling debts between 2019 and 2021. This should prevent confusion for your executors.

Please review the enclosed drafts and let me know if you are comfortable for us to proceed to formal signing next week.

Kind regards,

David Chan
Solicitor, Harbour Legal

metadata.subject: Updated Will and Bequest Intention – Execution Dates

artifacts.content:
Title: Phone call with Anna – bequest form queries
Date: 2025-11-26 11:30–12:00
Location: Home – kitchen phone
Attendees: Margaret; Anna (St Catherine’s Planned Giving)

Description:

Purpose of call: Ask Anna to walk me through confusing parts of the St Catherine’s bequest intention form before I fill it in properly.

Questions to cover:

1. “Personal details > dated” line
   - Clarify whether they want the date of my current will (19 February 2024), the date of my original 2017 will, or simply the date I complete and sign their form.
   - Ask if it matters that I might not sign the form on the same day that David and I formally execute the new codicil.

2. Flexibility of bequest amount
   - Explain that I’m comfortable indicating an approximate figure now, but that it may change. Mention Helen’s situation: the gambling debts from the Star Casino in Pyrmont (2019–2021) and the $65,000 consolidation loan I guaranteed in February 2024 at H&R Block in Randwick.
   - Check whether they prefer I attach a brief note outlining this, or if a sentence in the comments section is sufficient.

3. Acknowledgement preferences
   - Confirm whether I’m happy to be listed by name in any published donor lists, or if I’d rather remain anonymous until after my death.

Reminders for myself:

- Have the form, my will folder, and a pen ready on the table before the call.
- Jot down exactly what Anna says about the “dated” field so I don’t second‑guess it later.
- Offer to email her a scanned copy once I’ve completed everything in December.

metadata.title: Phone call with Anna – bequest form queries

artifacts.content:
From: Margaret Lawson <m.lawson52@gmail.com>
To: Development Office <giving@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au>
Date: 2025-01-22 10:14
Subject: Updated contact details for bequest file

Dear Alison,

Thank you again for taking the time to speak with me on the phone yesterday about updating my bequest paperwork. I realised afterward that the contact number you have on file for me is still the old home landline we disconnected when we changed internet providers.

Could you please update my record to show my mobile number as 0413 882 904? That’s the best way to reach me during the day, especially if I’m at the community centre exercise class or over in Maroubra visiting Mum. Robert still prefers the landline, but it’s in his name and often goes unanswered if we’re both out in the garden.

As we discussed, at present my bequest to St Catherine’s is contingent on my husband, Robert James Lawson, not needing to move into full-time high-care dementia accommodation at St Vincent’s Care Services in Maroubra. Since his mild cognitive impairment diagnosis at the Prince of Wales Hospital memory clinic in August 2022, our financial adviser at AMP in the city has warned that we may need an extra $250,000–$300,000 to cover potential bond and ongoing care fees, which could significantly reduce what is ultimately left to the School.

Please let me know if you need anything in writing from AMP or from our solicitor to note this contingency.

Warm regards,

Margaret Helen Lawson
12 Wentworth Street, Randwick NSW 2031
Mobile: 0413 882 904

metadata.subject: Updated contact details for bequest file

artifacts.content:
From: Emily Lawson <emily.lawson@health.nsw.gov.au>
To: Mum <m.lawson52@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-02-03 08:02
Subject: Can the school ring me instead?

Hi Mum,

I just got off the phone with the alumnae office at St Cath’s. They said they’d been trying to call you about the planned giving morning tea but kept getting a “number not connected” message.

I gave them my mobile, 0402 771 639, and told them that if they can’t reach you, they’re welcome to ring me and I’ll pass messages on. I know you hate answering unknown numbers on the home phone anyway, and half the time you’re out walking to Coogee or at tutoring when they try.

They mentioned you’d sent through some updated information about your bequest and that there were a couple of follow-up questions. I said you’re happy to talk, just that you’re juggling Gran in Maroubra, Robert’s appointments, and your tutoring kids, so sometimes it’s easier if they email first.

If you’re comfortable with it, I can also give them Robert’s mobile, but I suspect that will just confuse things, especially with everything going on since his memory clinic review. Maybe best to keep it simple and just use mine as the backup contact number.

Call me later (or text if you’re at exercise class) and I’ll fill you in properly.

Love,
Em

Emily Rose Lawson
Paediatric Physiotherapist
Mobile: 0402 771 639

Title: Phone appointment with AMP adviser re: aged care & bequest
Date: 2025-02-11 11:00–11:45
Location: Home – study (use cordless phone)
Attendees: Margaret Lawson; Daniel Price (AMP Financial Adviser)

Notes:

- Daniel to ring on home line first, then on mobile 0413 882 904 if no answer.
- Have most recent AMP portfolio statement printed and on desk.
- Questions to cover:
  • Clarify projected costs if Robert eventually needs to move into full-time high-care dementia accommodation at St Vincent’s Care Services in Maroubra.
  • Confirm Daniel’s estimate that we may need an extra $250,000–$300,000 available for potential bond and ongoing care fees, and how that might affect what is left in the estate.
  • Discuss how this contingency impacts the bequest wording for St Catherine’s – ensure it’s clear that support for Robert comes first.
  • Ask whether it’s better for the school to have my mobile number (0413 882 904) as primary contact rather than the landline, given how often I’m out.
- Remember to mention:
  • Robert’s latest memory clinic report from Prince of Wales Hospital (August 2022 diagnosis of mild cognitive impairment, but no major decline since).
  • That the school’s development office has been in touch about updating their records and want a reliable phone contact for me.

Reminder: Take call in study with door closed so Robert can watch his shows in the lounge without hearing all the financial details.

From: Sarah Lawson <s.lawson@harbourfamilylaw.com>

Subject: Draft will clauses + contact details for school

Mum,

Attached are the draft clauses from Helen (the solicitor, not Aunty Helen!) for your and Dad’s updated wills. I’ve highlighted the section dealing with the bequest to St Catherine’s and the contingency wording around Dad’s possible aged care needs.

Helen has asked that you double-check the contact information for the school in case they need to be notified by the executor. At the moment she has:

St Catherine’s School, Waverley
Attn: Director of Development
Phone: (02) 9369 1234
Email: development@stcatherines.nsw.edu.au

She said it’s not essential to have your own number in the clause itself, as that will be in the executor’s file, but if you want something noted, she can add “current contact details held on file with the School’s Development Office.”

Given everything with Dad’s mild cognitive impairment diagnosis and the conversations with AMP about possibly needing an extra $250,000–$300,000 for a bond at St Vincent’s Care Services in Maroubra, I think the way Helen has phrased the priority for his care before the school receives anything is sensible.

When you’ve read it, call me on my mobile (still 0411 588 230) and we can go through it together before you sign. I’ll also remind Helen to send a clean copy to your printer-friendly email.

Love,
Sarah

Sarah Louise Lawson
Senior Associate – Harbour Family Law
Mobile: 0411 588 230

Title: Call from St Catherine’s Development Office – confirm details
Date: 2025-03-05 15:30–16:00
Location: Home (kitchen phone)
Attendees: Margaret Lawson; Alison (St Catherine’s Development Office)

Description:

Alison to ring on mobile 0413 882 904 to confirm:

- Spelling of full name: Margaret Helen Lawson.
- Postal address: 12 Wentworth Street, Randwick NSW 2031.
- Best phone contact for school matters – advise that the mobile is usually better than the old landline, which Robert tends to ignore if he’s in the garden.
- Clarify that current bequest to St Catherine’s is contingent on Robert not needing to move into full-time high-care dementia accommodation at St Vincent’s Care Services in Maroubra.
- Note AMP adviser’s guidance that an additional $250,000–$300,000 may be required for potential bond and ongoing care fees, which could reduce the eventual bequest.
- Ask Alison to ensure my mobile number is correctly entered in their database and on any future event RSVP lists.

To prepare:

- Have folder with “Estate & Bequest – St Cath’s” on table next to phone.
- Put hearing aids in beforehand so I don’t miss anything.
- Jot down Alison’s direct line in case I need to ring her back (she mentioned a 9369 number last time, but I didn’t catch the last four digits).

Reminder: Move kettle off the boil before 3:30 so the noise doesn’t drown out the call.

metadata.title: Call from St Catherine’s Development Office – confirm details

contains_negative_info: Husband potential need for high-care dementia accommodation; possible reduction of bequest

artifacts.content:
Dear Admissions Office,

I’m helping my granddaughter Bella with a form for the upcoming St Catherine’s music scholarship audition and we’re a bit stuck on the “Emergency Contact Phone” section. Could you please confirm whether you prefer a landline or mobile number there? I usually give out our home phone, (02) 9381 0043, because I’m almost always at home in Randwick during school hours, but I’m not sure if the school prefers a mobile in case we’re out.

Part of my hesitation is that I’ve been updating various documents with different contact numbers lately. My solicitor at McKenzie & Co. in Randwick has just revised my will to reflect that my bequest to St Catherine’s is currently set at 40% of my estate, but may need to be reduced depending on how my daughter Sarah’s family law matter in the Federal Circuit and Family Court turns out. If her settlement leaves her and the children in a precarious position, I want to make sure they can stay in their Rose Bay home and keep up with school fees.

Because of these changes, I’ve been careful to double‑check that the right phone number is listed for me on all school‑related records. I don’t want you to have an outdated number if there’s ever an emergency with any of the grandchildren while they’re on campus.

Warm regards,

Margaret Helen Lawson
12 Wentworth Street
Randwick NSW 2031
Home: (02) 9381 0043
Mobile: 0412 889 563

metadata.subject: Clarification about preferred emergency contact phone number

artifacts.content:
Hi Emily darling,

Can you please remind the school nurse to update my contact details on Thomas’s file? At the parent–grandparent meeting last term they still had the old Coogee landline we disconnected years ago. For anything to do with Thomas’s anxiety or if he has another episode of school refusal and needs to be picked up early, they should ring our Randwick home phone on (02) 9381 7752 first, and if there’s no answer, try my mobile 0403 221 947.

I’ve had to be quite particular about phone numbers lately because I’m juggling calls from the Sydney Children’s Hospital, his psychologist, and McKenzie & Co. Solicitors. I’ve told the solicitor that if Thomas is still struggling by the time he reaches senior school, up to $80,000 of the amount I’d set aside in my will for St Catherine’s may instead go toward his psychological treatment and any specialist tutoring he needs until he finishes Year 12. There are so many moving parts that I don’t want messages going to the wrong number.

If you like, I can jot these numbers on a sticky note for his year adviser as well when I’m next at the school. It might save confusion when they’re trying to work out whether to call you, me, or Robert.

Love,
Mum

Margaret H. Lawson
Home: (02) 9381 7752
Mobile: 0403 221 947

Appointment with: McKenzie & Co. Solicitors – Estate & Contact Details Review

Notes:

Meet with Julia at McKenzie & Co. in Randwick to go over:

1. Bequest structure for St Catherine’s
   - Confirm that the school currently stands to receive 40% of my estate.
   - Reiterate that this percentage may need to be adjusted depending on the outcome of Sarah’s property settlement in the Federal Circuit and Family Court.
   - Ask Julia to double‑check that the explanatory letter in my file clearly notes the priority of ensuring Sarah, Isabella and Jamie can remain in their Rose Bay home and keep up school fees if things go badly.

2. Update contact details
   - Ensure my primary contact number is listed as the Randwick home phone: (02) 9381 9920, with Robert’s mobile 0415 667 203 as secondary.
   - Remove the old tutoring mobile number that I rarely use now.
   - Confirm that Mum’s facility (Southern Cross Care Maroubra) has the correct number for me as well, as they still sometimes ring the out‑of‑date line.

3. Patricia’s care costs
   - Explain that since Mum’s fall in October 2023 and her move into high‑care, I’ve been paying an extra $1,450 per fortnight for her accommodation and additional services.
   - Ask whether further increases might require another amendment to the will.

Bring: current will, list of phone numbers, latest invoice from Southern Cross Care.

metadata.title: Solicitor – will and phone contact update

artifacts.content:
Dear Southern Cross Care Maroubra Admin Team,

I’m writing to check that you have my correct contact details on file for my mother, Patricia Anne Carter (DOB: 1932). Since her fall in October 2023 and subsequent move into high‑care, I’ve been receiving a few messages via my old tutoring mobile rather than the home phone, which makes it easy to miss important updates about her care.

Could you please update her records so that my primary contact number is our home landline, (02) 9381 3105, and my secondary contact is my mobile, 0411 584 299. My husband Robert is usually at home with me, so if you can’t reach me directly, he will be able to pass on any urgent information.

As you know, I’m currently paying an additional $1,450 per fortnight towards Mum’s accommodation and extra services from my own savings. I’ve also structured my will so that St Catherine’s School will only receive the full intended bequest if Mum does not outlive me; otherwise, more of my estate will need to be directed to her ongoing care. Because of this, it’s vital that I’m easy to contact if there are any changes to her health status or to the fees.

Please confirm once the phone numbers have been updated, and let me know if there are any other forms I should complete.

With thanks,

Margaret Helen Lawson
12 Wentworth Street
Randwick NSW 2031
Home: (02) 9381 3105
Mobile: 0411 584 299

metadata.subject: Update of contact phone numbers for Patricia Anne Carter

artifacts.content:
Event: Phone check & emergency contact audit

Description:

Set aside time to go through all the places my phone number appears and make sure they’re current, especially for anything to do with the grandchildren and St Catherine’s.

Tasks:

• Call St Catherine’s reception to confirm which number they have listed as my emergency contact for Bella and Jamie. Ask them to replace the old Coogee landline with my current Randwick home phone: (02) 9381 5528.

• Email Emily and Sarah a short list of which numbers schools and doctors should use for me (home first, then mobile 0402 998 431) so we’re all giving out the same details on forms.

• Check with McKenzie & Co. that the estate documents include the correct contact numbers next to the sections about the 40% bequest to the School and the clause allowing up to $80,000 to be diverted to Thomas’s psychological treatment and specialist tutoring if needed.

• Ring Southern Cross Care Maroubra to verify that they’ve updated my number on Mum’s file since I started paying the extra $1,450 per fortnight for her high‑care accommodation and services.

Notes:

Do this before the next term starts so all school records are up to date.

Reminder: Take the little address book from the kitchen drawer so I can cross out the old numbers.
